item.title: Containment Procedures
item.content:encoded: See, I really like this, but I can't upvote it due to the fact there is nothing about how he is actually contained in that section. It feels like it's not a properly written report without that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done. The Democratic Republic of Goatistan approves. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, this is the kind of content that keeps me coming back to the site. This is a quirky, original idea. It’s executed quite excellently. It doesn’t shirk in horror. And it’s just all-around great.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I upvoted after reading the Special Containment Procedures. Then I read the rest and tried to upvote again, but the site wouldn't let me. Well done, this is great.

item.title: Euclid?
item.content:encoded:
I know initially he seems only Euclid, but the idea of this guy is he is an entire country, right? He himself contains an assumed distopian country. So, his idea is he has all the powers a country has… in human form.

Since he has been shown to be able to summon government officials… I was thinking, he quite possibly has an entire army on his side. Think about it, the last time war was declared, almost immediately an official came in and dealt with the situation, making an offer about terms of surrender. The Foundation, seeing it as the easy path, took it.

But… If they didn't, I imagine that this guy could summon armies to his rescue. I mean, he is a country. It has already been shown that he has people inside of him, wouldn't it make sense that the Republic of Arnold Fitzwilliams has a military, too?

I think that people have played around with the idea of how powerful it would be for the Foundation to have an entire country under their thumb, but I'm not sure people have taken into consideration how horrible it would be to make enemies with this guy. If I were a scientist in this situation, I'd be very cautious about how I handled this guy's needs…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incident 1761-XXX

After several days of prolonged war with SCP-1761, Foundation losses are as follows:

███ anonymous deaths of Foundation MTF and Security personnel
Sites 19, 31, ██, 234
████ anonymous deaths of other Foundation personnel

Site ██ remains under assault. Foundation held territory is down to █%.

While Foundation diplomats continue to work with SCP-1761-2 instances to negotiate a cease fire, they report substantial war reparations have been demanded.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"SCP FAM Radio Episode 5", by Toad King Studios, has an interview with the Republic at ~17 minutes.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=HXqBoTTt0aw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Proposed alternate containment method:
The Foundation declares war on him until it is able to claim that it has conquered the Republic of Arnold Fitzwilliams. SCP-1761 is reclassified as Site-Arnold Fitzwilliams and is brought completely under Foundation control. The Foundation now owns (another?) country and can use it to gain resources, including the use of Fitzwillian death-row criminals as D-Class, and possible recruits for other Foundation positions. Oh, and I feel the need to restate that the Foundation would have a whole country under their thumb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It went from original and silly to scary as hell in an instant. I love it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why don't they test it by sending somebody in with a tourist visa?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Love it. +1.

item.title: Oh dear
item.content:encoded:
Even though I helped you write and polish this SCP, something that never occurred to me until just the other day, and that I therefore didn't ask…

… is this inspired by Hetalia?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I burst out laughing and upvoted on that annexing of containment chamber.

item.title: Incidentally
item.content:encoded:
This passage here —

Dr. █████: You’re avoiding my questions.

SCP-1761-1: The Republic of Arnold Fitzwilliams supports human rights and freedom of information.

— you know what this means?

This means "fuck you, of course I'm avoiding your questions, you're holding me captive and keeping everything about me a secret from the world. I support human rights and therefore wouldn't keep someone like me locked up. I support freedom of information and therefore wouldn't keep something like this a secret."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Congrats on a great first SCP! I would love to see the Foundation try to negotiate an exchange student program. I think that would be a good way to get more information on what it's like "there."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great, quirky, and humorous, and then it gets dark really, really fast. I love it!
At +100, The Republic of Arnold Fitzwilliams joins the UN.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very good. I'm reminded a bit of the company-that's-a-person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: … Why can't I upvote comments?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No man is an island, but at least one is clearly a sovereign nation state.

Wtf dude, this is brilliant. 80 is too low.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interaction with SCP-1761-1 and SCP-1761-2 must be approved and monitored by a Foundation diplomat.

I'm sorry to barge in like this, but THIS SENTENCE NEEDS A TALE. : D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is excellent. I like it a lot. I wish I was my own country.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it very much, but something is nagging me. There is no mention about how this SCP came to the Foundation's hands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant, concise, well-executed. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anomalous diplomacy is always interesting. Why fight against the darkness when you can negotiate?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm bugged by seeing "an 1761-delta event". Why is it "an" when all logic and reason demand it should be "a"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This needs to be at +100 right the hell now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well-written, interesting and unique. Great job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're probably right. Removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am emphatically seconding this. The premise is good, but the list of countries is just ridiculous and makes my eyes glaze over to look at. If there's something worthwhile hidden in there, I'm given no reason to search for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Love this. Also I automatically upvote anything that would make even the most hardened Foundation researchers throw up their hands and just say, "okay, what the fuck is going on here?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really great.
I read the description and instantly thought of Thoreau: "No man in America has ever stood up so persistently and effectively for the dignity of human nature, knowing himself for a man, and the equal of any and all governments."
(http://thoreau.eserver.org/plea1.html), paragraph 36, if someone wants to read it up.
Then I read the interviews and wtf?
And then I read the last interview and thought: The foundation really should think about the 'only D-Class testing' policy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love it when a first SCP succeeds.

Have an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, was this inspired by the whole sovereign citizen thing?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is quirky, original, and hilarious. Fantastic. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very cool. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A country…that is a person…that appears to be a dystopia.

I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is something I always wanted that I never knew I wanted until now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is extraordinary for a first run. Well done, and welcome aboard!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the good kind of weird. I am a fan.

I'd recommend trimming the lists of countries out of the -2 descriptions. They take up an awful lot of space without actually adding anything to the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last interview log is excellent. Well done! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first SCP! Credit to Voct for editing and general advice.
